lessonTitle: Health Maintenance in Sickle Cell Disease

items.html: Define health maintenance in caring for patients with Sickle Cell Disease
List both pharmacological and non pharmacological ways of dealing with the pain associated with SCD
Identify ways PCPs can partner with haematologists in the comprehensive health maintenance and HU adherence for patients with SCD

data.html: Before we jump into health maintenance for individuals with SCD, it's important to acknowledge the important role our primary care colleagues provide to promote the health of all patients. This includes routine health screening (for cancers, cardiovascular disease, etc), vaccinations, and comprehensive care that improves health outcomes in individuals with or without SCD.

slides.title: Patient's Voice: Benefits of PCPs Collaborating in Care

data.html:
There is a great deal of appreciation for our PCP partners and this patient wanted to share about her positive experiences she has had her PCP.

After listening, take a moment to reflect and think about one thing you are taking away with you after listening to Catherine's testimonial.

slides.title: What is Health Maintenance in SCD?

data.html: PCPs can partner with haematologists and the patient to help maintain the best health quality of life possible for patients with SCD. Monitoring for signs of end organ damage, intervening early, and minimizing acute pain episodes will help contribute to improved outcomes. Some ways PCPs can lead and support care by:

items.html: Ordering recommended imaging and laboratory tests
Referring patients to the appropriate specialists (e.g. – ophthalmology, nephrology, etc.)
Help maintain vaccinations and adherent to prophylactic antibiotics (e.g. – penicillin VK)

data.html: Common complications for individuals with SCD include:
The complications above can have an early onset, but the actual incidence with those complications increases with age.

data.question: There are medicinal and non medicinal ways of treating pain associated with SCD
data.feedback_correct: It is imperative to incorporate both medicinal and non medicinal ways of treating the pain associated with SCD.

items.html: To maximize outcomes for individuals with SCD, completing their regular health maintenance evaluations is essential.
To care for patients holistically, we must try pharmacological, non pharmacological, and adjunct therapies to be more wholesome in our approach
Primary care providers are key partners in helping in the management of acute and chronic pain, as comfort grows as a clinician.
Hehre, R.J. (2020). Managing patients with sickle cell disease in primary care. Journal of the American Academy of PAs
Onimoe, G., & Rotz, S. 2020. Sickle cell disease: A primary care update. Cleveland Clinic Journal of Medicine, 87(1)
Humphreys, J. V. (2012). The Management of Sickle Cell Disease in a Primary Care Setting. Journal of Family Medicine and Primary Care Volume 1 : Issue 1
